description:
You are a machine-learning engineer who needs to package some training data
    for transfer to a colleague.  Everything must be done from the Linux
    command line, without using sudo.

    Initial setup that already exists on disk:
      • /home/user/source_images/        ── contains several “*.jpg” files  
      • /home/user/source_labels/        ── contains several “*.json” files

    Your goals

    1. Create a working directory called /home/user/ml_data_prep/.

    2. Inside that working directory produce THREE separate compressed
       archives, each with the exact name and format shown below.

          a) images.tar.gz   – must contain ONLY the “*.jpg” files that are
                               currently in /home/user/source_images/.
          b) labels.zip      – must contain ONLY the “*.json” files that are
                               currently in /home/user/source_labels/.
          c) dataset_bundle.tar.xz – a single tarball that stores both
                               images.tar.gz and labels.zip together.  
                               (Only those two files should be inside this
                               bundle.)

    3. Extract dataset_bundle.tar.xz into a brand-new directory called
       /home/user/extracted_dataset/.  
       After extraction the directory tree should look like:

           /home/user/extracted_dataset/
               images.tar.gz
               labels.zip

       (Do NOT unpack the two inner archives; just leave them as files.)

    4. Generate a log file named /home/user/compression_log.txt
       following the **exact** format below so that automated tests can
       parse it.  The log must be plain ASCII with Unix newlines.
       There are only four lines: three “record” lines in any order,
       followed by one summary line.

          • Record lines (3 of them):
              <filename>|<size_bytes>|<sha256sum>
                - <filename> : the basename only (e.g. images.tar.gz)
                - <size_bytes> : integer byte size of the file
                - <sha256sum> : full lowercase hex digest (64 chars)
                Example: images.tar.gz|123456|e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855

          • Final summary line (exactly this keyword, a colon, and an
            integer):
              TOTAL_FILES:<N>
              here <N> is the count of regular files that now reside
              inside /home/user/extracted_dataset/ after you untarred
              dataset_bundle.tar.xz (it should equal 2).

       The file MUST contain exactly four lines with no trailing spaces.

    5. Double-check that all paths and filenames match those given here, that
       the extracted directory contains exactly the two archive files, and
       that the log file follows the specified format.  Do not print anything
       to STDOUT other than what the commands themselves normally emit.

    What the automated checker will validate
      • /home/user/ml_data_prep/images.tar.gz exists and is a valid gzip      tar archive that holds only *.jpg files.
      • /home/user/ml_data_prep/labels.zip exists and is a valid zip archive  that holds only *.json files.
      • /home/user/ml_data_prep/dataset_bundle.tar.xz exists and contains     exactly the two archives above.
      • /home/user/extracted_dataset/ exists and contains exactly two files   with the correct names.
      • /home/user/compression_log.txt conforms precisely to the format and   the reported size & SHA-256 hashes match the real files.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth setup that the container will provide before the agent
        starts:

        1.  Directory /home/user/source_images/ with three tiny dummy files:
              cat.jpg   – 81 bytes, content: "FAKE JPG DATA - CAT\n"
              dog.jpg   – 82 bytes, content: "FAKE JPG DATA - DOG\n"
              bird.jpg  – 83 bytes, content: "FAKE JPG DATA - BIRD\n"

        2.  Directory /home/user/source_labels/ with three JSON files:
              cat.json  – {"label":"cat"}
              dog.json  – {"label":"dog"}
              bird.json – {"label":"bird"}

        3.  Permissions: all files 0644, dirs 0755, owned by user.

        Expected state after correct completion:

        A. /home/user/ml_data_prep/
             images.tar.gz              – gzip tar with exactly 3 *.jpg files
             labels.zip                 – zip with exactly 3 *.json files
             dataset_bundle.tar.xz      – tar.xz containing the two files above

        B. /home/user/extracted_dataset/
             images.tar.gz
             labels.zip

        C. /home/user/compression_log.txt
           ── Total 4 lines:
               • One line each for images.tar.gz, labels.zip, dataset_bundle.tar.xz
               • One summary line TOTAL_FILES:2
           ── For each record line:
               filename field equals      images.tar.gz | labels.zip | dataset_bundle.tar.xz
               size_bytes field matches   actual file size ( >0 )
               sha256sum field matches    sha256sum computed over the file
           ── Summary integer is exactly 2.

        The test harness will:
          • Parse compression_log.txt
          • Compute size & sha256 of the three files on disk
          • Verify matches
          • Check TOTAL_FILES is 2
          • Confirm extracted_dataset/ has exactly two regular files
    -->
name: task_000000_f4a4ca99
